checks.finding: REQ-001 specifies exact deletion semantics (whole line + terminator, column-0 anchor, any field value, LF/CRLF, task-stage-only scope, sh/ps1 byte-parity) and is fully covered by AC-001..AC-004 with concrete exit-code/output/diagnostic assertions in acceptance-tests.md TEST-001..004; Edge Cases in requirements.md enumerate the boundary conditions (quoted-text masking, indented non-matches, multiple lines, pre-existing line, no-trailing-newline) an implementer/verifier needs.
The single stated goal REQ-001 is traced to AC-001 through AC-004, each explicitly tagged '(REQ-001)', and mapped 1:1 to TEST-001..004; no goal lacks a corresponding acceptance criterion.

AC-001/AC-002 specify exit codes and exact diagnostic substrings ('workflow-state: ok', 'stage-provenance'/'task plan hash is stale'); AC-003 specifies byte-identical normalized forms; AC-004 specifies identical exit statuses/first diagnostic rule IDs and a file-verifiable registration in tests/run-all.sh - all externally observable.

requirements.md 'Non-goals' explicitly excludes check-task-state content validation, hook-guard rule changes, spec-stage/impl-stage normalization, identity ledger, registry schema, other validators, and retroactive re-hashing of existing contracts, bounding the fix to task-stage normalization in the two named validator twins.

'Security Boundaries' states the column-0/exact-prefix anchoring constraint and compensating controls (hook guard, check-task-state) that remain in force; 'Roles and Permissions' constrains live-path application to a human-copy procedure with a SHA-256 manifest (per Risks); 'Assumptions' states the pwsh 7.x and --registry entry-point dependencies material to testing.

Three risks in 'Risks' (over-broad masking, sh/ps1 normal-form divergence, botched live apply to protected gate scripts) each carry an explicit mitigation tied to a specific acceptance criterion (AC-002; AC-003/AC-004) or process control (staged TDD, SHA-256 manifest, existing suite re-run at gate).
